title: grammica gymnocarpa
side_effects: Grammica gymnocarpa can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat and decreased blood pressure. In severe cases, ingestion of large amounts can cause seizures, coma, or even death, highlighting the importance of proper handling and dosage. Additionally, improper use may also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory distress.
